question_text: A wooden block floats in water, but a steel ball sinks. Which statement best explains why?

options.label: B
options.text: Wood is less dense than water, but steel is more dense than water

options.label: C
options.text: Wood repels water but steel attracts it

options.label: D
options.text: Steel is heavier, so it always sinks

explanation: Objects float when they are less dense than the fluid they're in, and sink when they're more dense. Wood has a lower density than water (around 600 kg/m³), while steel is much denser (around 7800 kg/m³), so it sinks. The weight alone doesn't matter—it's the density that determines floating.
